data.description:
User feedback (2026-04-17): the right fix is not to generate KnotState from loom bundles — it is to delete the enum entirely. Nothing in the codebase branches on a specific variant for business logic; every capability the enum provides is workflow knowledge that already lives (or belongs) in loom/*/dist/bundle.json.

Current state (src/domain/state.rs):
  * Hand-maintained enum with 22 variants, ALL[], as_str(), FromStr with legacy aliases, is_terminal(), can_transition_to() transition table.
  * Non-test consumers outside state.rs: profile_commands.rs, tiering.rs, write_dispatch/helpers.rs, state_hierarchy.rs, app/error.rs. None match on a specific variant for business logic; they only (a) validate a string is known, (b) ask is_terminal, or (c) look up a rank.

Why a closed enum is wrong, not just stale:
  * The enum is the union of every installed workflow's states. Installing a workflow with a genuinely new state name fails FromStr — so today the system cannot actually honor its own 'pluggable workflows' premise; it only accepts workflows whose states are already compiled in.
  * Generating the enum from bundles (prior proposal) preserves that surface and just automates sync. It does not remove the constraint.

Target design:
  * Replace KnotState enum with a KnotState(String) newtype that carries only the canonical state name.
  * Validation, terminality, ordering, and allowed transitions become queries against the installed Workflow loaded from loom/*/dist/bundle.json.
  * Terminal-ness: bundle attribute on each state. Cold-tier logic in tiering.rs consults the workflow, not a hardcoded set.
  * Rank/ordering: bundle-defined order. Delete the hardcoded 0–16 table in state_hierarchy.rs.
  * Transitions: already in the bundle as edges. Delete can_transition_to's hardcoded match.
  * Legacy aliases (idea, work_item, implemented, etc.): declared in the bundle (or a migrations file alongside it), not hardcoded in FromStr.

Scope:
  * Remove KnotState enum, ALL, as_str, is_terminal, can_transition_to, FromStr hardcoded mapping.
  * Introduce Workflow-aware helpers that take &Workflow and a state name and answer the questions above.
  * Update tiering.rs, state_hierarchy.rs, write_dispatch/helpers.rs, profile_commands.rs, app/error.rs, and tests to use the workflow-driven API.
  * Keep a ParseKnotStateError-style error meaning 'state not declared by this knot's workflow.'

Out of scope:
  * Changing the bundle schema beyond adding any missing attributes (terminal, order, aliases) needed to replace hardcoded knowledge.
  * Cross-workflow state merging: each knot's states are interpreted against its own workflow; no global alphabet is needed.
